data.title: Paulson & Co. takes new position in Solstice Advanced Materials, exits Trilogy Metals in Q4 moves
data.description:
Investment firm Paulson & Co. took a new stake in Solstice Advanced Materials (SOLS) and exited its position in Trilogy Metals (TMQ), among its fourth quarter moves.

Founded by John Paulson in 1994 and headquartered in New York City, the hedge fund disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing published on Tuesday.

The firm took 1.4M shares in Solstice Advanced Materials, while increasing its position in Bausch Health Companies (BHC) to 73.26M shares from 70.76M shares, as per the filing.

Meanwhile, Paulson & Co. decreased its stake in Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL) to 1.71M shares from 1.91M shares.

data.title: Starboard Value initiates new positions in CWAN, FLR, exits Alight among Q4 moves
data.description:
Jeff Smith's Starboard Value established new holdings in Clearwater Analytics (CWAN) and Fluor Corporation (FLR) and exited Alight (ALIT), while increasing its stakes in Kenvue (KVUE) and Riot Platforms (RIOT), among its Q4 moves.

Starboard Value disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing published on Tuesday.

During the quarter, the fund snapped up 9.95M shares of Clearwater Analytics (CWAN) and 5.19M shares of Fluor Corporation (FLR). On the other hand, it closed its position in Alight (ALIT), which stood at 45.86M shares in the previous quarter.

The fund also strengthened its positions in Kenvue (KVUE) to 27.31M shares, valued at $471.06M, from 20.93M shares in the previous quarter.

According to the 13F filing, it also increased its holdings in Riot Platforms (RIOT) to 12.74M shares from 8.19M shares, Match Group (MTCH) to 11.4M shares from 11.07M shares, and Tripadvisor (TRIP) to 9.64M shares from 9.51M shares in the previous quarter.

On the other hand, Starboard Value trimmed its positions in Autodesk (ADSK) from 1.31M shares to 1M shares and in Salesforce (CRM) from 1.24M to 940.32K.

The fund also reduced its holdings in Becton, Dickinson and Company (BDX) from 1.21M shares to 929.92K shares and in Gen Digital (GEN) from 12.17M shares to 10.58M shares. Its stake was also decreased in Healthcare Realty Trust (HR) from 14.49M shares to 12.6M shares and in Rogers Corporation (ROG) from 1.68M shares to 1.20M shares during the quarter.

data.title: Glenview Capital's top Q4 moves include closing out PYPL, PINS stakes, boosting LION
data.description:
Glenview Capital dumped its holdings in PayPal Holdings (PYPL), Pinterest (PINS), Marvell Technology (MRVL) and Alight (ALIT) during the fourth quarter of last year, the hedge fund disclosed on Tuesday.

It also trimmed its positions in United Airlines Holdings (UAL), to 511K shares from 582K shares in Q3, and Expedia Group (EXPE), to 322K from 360K.

Conversely, the firm opened new positions in Genius Sports (GENI), with 2.03M shares, and Six Flags Entertainment (FUN), with 1.12M shares, during the three-month period.

And it increased its stakes in Lionsgate Studios (LION), to 7.51M shares from 5M shares in Q3, and Global Payments (GPN), to 5.92M from 5.14M, the 13F filing showed.

data.title: ValueAct Capital adds positions in RKT, TOST, while cutting DIS and NSIT, among other Q4 moves
data.description:
ValueAct Capital, a San Francisco-based investment management firm with about $10 billion in assets under management, made significant changes to its portfolio in the fourth quarter, dramatically increasing its stakes in fintech companies while trimming positions in media and enterprise services, according to its latest 13F filing.

The most notable move was ValueAct’s aggressive expansion of its Rocket Companies (RKT) position, adding nearly 14M shares to bring its total holdings to 39,380,652 shares. The firm also substantially increased its bet on restaurant technology platform Toast (TOST), boosting its stake by 4,618,077 shares to reach 8,015,277 shares.

Additionally, ValueAct added 1,692,600 shares of gaming platform Roblox (RBLX), bringing that position to 5,975,821 shares. The quarter also saw ValueAct establish new positions in BlackRock (BLK) with 695,000 shares and Liberty Live Holdings (LLYVK) with 1,799,319 shares.

On the other side of the ledger, ValueAct made significant cuts to several holdings. The firm reduced its Disney (DIS) stake by 1,314,000 shares, leaving it with 3,077,931 shares.

Insight Enterprises (NSIT) saw one of the largest reductions, with ValueAct trimming more than 1M shares to bring its position down to 1,216,592 shares. The firm also reduced its MongoDB (MDB) holdings by 711,400 shares to 1,406,800 shares and completely exited its position in Mr. Cooper Group, which had stood at 904,800 shares in Q3.

ValueAct made more modest adjustments elsewhere in its portfolio, adding 96,000 shares of Salesforce (CRM), 264,200 shares of Simpson Manufacturing (SSD), 182,200 shares of Live Nation Entertainment (LYV), 279,300 shares of Meta Platforms (META), and 152,400 shares of Visa (V).

The firm slightly trimmed its Amazon (AMZN) position by 106,200 shares while keeping its CBRE Group (CBRE) stake unchanged at 234,591 shares.

data.title: West Pharmaceutical announces up to $1B share repurchase program
data.description:
• West Pharmaceutical Services (WST) said its board has authorized an up to $1B share repurchase program.
  • The company exhausted a prior buyback that had an expiration date of Dec. 31.

data.title: Donnelley Financial expects 80% of 2026 revenue to be recurring amid software growth and AI investments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Donnelley Financial Solutions (DFIN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Daniel Leib, President, CEO & Director, reported "strong fourth quarter results, highlighted by 10.4% consolidated net sales growth, year-over-year growth in adjusted EBITDA and strong adjusted EBITDA margin." He emphasized double-digit growth in software solutions and event-driven transactional offerings as key contributors and highlighted an accelerated share buyback of approximately 1.3 million shares in Q4, totaling 3.6 million shares repurchased in 2025.
  • Leib announced DFIN's transition from "Chapter 2" (transformation phase) to "Chapter 3," focused on sustained growth in 2026, stating, "With revenue from recurring and reoccurring offerings approaching 80% of our full year total revenue...we expect the evolution of our revenue profile towards a higher mix of predictable revenue to continue going forward."
  • He pointed to record Software Solutions net sales of $358.4 million in 2025, up 8.7% from 2024, and noted that Software Solutions now comprise approximately 47% of total net sales. Key product updates included "ActiveDisclosure" sales up 17% for the year, and "Arc Suite" net sales up 11%, with ArcFlex launched to capture private investment reporting opportunities.
  • AI featured prominently, with Leib stating, "DFIN is deploying AI across both our product offerings as well as our internal operations," including the Active Intelligence suite within ActiveDisclosure, aiming to streamline compliance and reporting.
  • David Gardella, Executive VP & CFO, stated, "We delivered strong fourth quarter results in an uncertain operating environment, including double-digit consolidated year-over-year net sales growth, higher adjusted EBITDA, adjusted EBITDA margin expansion and an increase in both operating cash flow and free cash flow from last year's fourth quarter."

Outlook

  • Management guided for consolidated Q1 2026 net sales in the range of $200 million to $210 million and adjusted EBITDA margin of 33% to 35%. Gardella explained, "the midpoint of our consolidated revenue guidance, $205 million, implies an increase of approximately 2% as the decline in print and distribution volume will be more than offset by software solutions sales growth."
  • Transactional sales for Q1 2026 are expected in the range of $45 million to $50 million, with the midpoint approximately flat compared to Q1 2025.
  • Capital spending for 2026 is projected between $55 million and $60 million, aligned with 2025 levels.
  • Leib indicated "we do not expect major SEC rule changes for 2026," and expects print and distribution to continue to decline, offset by Software Solutions growth.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated net sales for Q4 2025 were $172.5 million, an increase of $16.2 million or 10.4% year-over-year. Software Solutions net sales grew 11.4% year-over-year.
  • Adjusted non-GAAP gross margin was 63.5%, up 360 basis points, and adjusted EBITDA was $45.8 million with a margin of 26.6%, up 630 basis points from Q4 2024.
  • Segment details: Capital Markets Software Solutions net sales were $60 million (20% growth); Capital Markets, Compliance and Communications Management net sales were $61.6 million (15.6% growth); and Investment Company Software Solutions were $30.9 million (down 2.2%).
  • Free cash flow in Q4 was $47.9 million and $107.8 million for the full year.
  • Share repurchases totaled approximately 3.6 million shares for $172.3 million in 2025.

Q&A

  • Charles Strauzer, CJS Securities, Inc.: Asked about the drivers behind Q4 outperformance and margin strength. Gardella responded that "price was not significant. I'd say a modest driver. Really, when we think about the outperformance, much of it was on the capital markets transactional revenue...predominantly volume."
  • Strauzer asked about margin drivers. Gardella replied, "the mix of sales is changing, what we're doing with the cost structure...the incremental margin on the sales growth has really pushed margin higher."
  • Strauzer inquired about capital allocation and valuation multiples. Leib indicated expectations have not adjusted quickly and signaled continued monitoring of opportunities: "if valuations persist at a lower level with the AI overhang, then I think they will."
  • Peter Heckmann, D.A. Davidson & Co.: Queried the TAM and integration opportunity for ArcFlex. Leib confirmed ArcFlex can be stand-alone or synergistic and highlighted growing demand in private funds. Eric Johnson, President of Global Investment Companies, added "private fund numbers...early 2025 was over 54,000, up 15% from the prior period 2023."
  • Heckmann asked about IPO activity and DFIN's share. Craig Clay, President of Global Capital Markets, stated "Q4's volume was up 70% year-over-year in the $100 million category...our share...was 65%."
  • Unknown Analyst (Needham & Company): Asked about the sustainability of double-digit software growth in 2026. Gardella explained that "we expect continued strong growth from ActiveDisclosure and Venue heading into '26" but Arc Suite growth will be "more lumpy...tied to new regulation."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on drivers of outperformance, sustainability of margins, capital allocation, and product growth, with a generally positive and inquisitive tone.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, highlighting execution and strategic clarity. Gardella and Leib used language such as "feel really good about the direction where we're going with profitability" and "I'm confident with our continued focus on executing our strategy."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's confidence remained high, but was more forward-looking in describing the transition to a new strategic phase and recurring revenue mix targets. Analysts' tone was similarly positive but more focused on sustainability and product specifics.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • In Q4, management shifted from transformation to a sustained growth strategy, explicitly targeting recurring revenues to approach 80% of total revenue in 2026, compared to a prior focus on software sales mix and resilience amid market softness.
  • Guidance language strengthened, with a clear range for Q1 2026 net sales and adjusted EBITDA margin provided, versus more caution in Q3 due to government shutdown uncertainty.
  • Key metrics improved: Q4 net sales and adjusted EBITDA margin both increased versus Q3, with significant growth in Capital Markets segments and continued investment in AI and product development.
  • Analysts in Q4 were more focused on sustainability and future growth opportunities, especially in software and private markets, compared to Q3's concerns over shutdown impact and operational resilience.
  • Management's sentiment evolved from resilience and adaptation in Q3 to confidence in sustained growth and profitability in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the secular decline in print and distribution revenue, expected to continue, though offset by software growth.
  • Market-driven risks include timing and volume of capital markets transactions, with Gardella noting "recent market volatility has the potential to impact the timing of certain transactions between quarters."
  • No major SEC regulatory changes anticipated for 2026, potentially resulting in "more modest growth" for Arc Suite outside regulatory-driven surges.
  • Analysts probed the durability of margin expansion and variability of software segment growth.

Final Takeaway

DFIN closed 2025 with robust top- and bottom-line performance, propelled by double-digit software solutions growth, a favorable shift in revenue mix, and ongoing cost discipline. The company is entering a new strategic phase in 2026, targeting a higher mix of recurring revenue, continued strong profitability, and sustained cash flow generation, underpinned by investments in AI-driven solutions, product innovation, and a disciplined capital allocation approach.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Toll Brothers Q1 earnings beat even as per-home price disappoints
data.description:
Toll Brothers (TOL) delivered fiscal Q1 earnings and revenue that topped Wall Street consensus even as its average price per home delivered missed the average analyst estimate. The company, for the most part, maintained its guidance for the full year. 

The homebuilder reaffirmed its full-year guidance for 10.3K-10.7K home deliveries  (vs. Visible Alpha consensus of 10.6K) and the average delivered  price per home at $970K-$990K (Visible Alpha estimate of $983K). It lowered its outlook for adjusted home sales gross margin to 25.5% from its previous guidance of 26.00%.

For fiscal Q2, the company expects to deliver 2,400-2,500 homes vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of 2,495; an average delivered per-home price of $975K-$985K vs. the $977.4K Visible Alpha estimate; and an adjusted home sales gross margin of 26.0% vs. 26.5% reported in Q1.

Fiscal Q1 2026 GAAP EPS of $2.19, beating the average analyst estimate of $2.11, rose from $1.75 in Q1 2025.

Revenue of $2.15B for the quarter ended Feb. 28, 2026, topping the $1.86B consensus, increased from $1.86B in the year-ago quarter.

"We continue to be very pleased with our focus on the luxury market and its more affluent customer base," said Chairman and CEO Douglas C. Yearley, Jr. "We also continue to benefit from our broad geographic footprint, the widest variety of home offerings and price points in the industry, and our balanced mix of build-to-order and spec homes."

The average per-home price of $976.8K, lagging the $989.4K Visible Alpha estimate, climbed from $924.6K in last year’s Q1.

Q1 homes delivered fell to 1,899 from 1,991 a year ago and compared with the Visible Alpha estimate of 1,852.

Backlog value of $6.02B at Feb. 28, 2026, fell from $6.94B a year ago. Homes in backlog dropped to 5,051 vs. 6,312 in Q1 2025.

Q1 adjusted home sales gross margin, as a percentage of home sales revenue, shrank  to 26.5% from 26.9% in last year’s Q1.

Cancellation rate, as a percentage of signed contracts, declined to 5.4% in Q1 from 5.8% in the year-ago period.

Toll Brothers (TOL) stock slipped 0.4% in after-hours trading.

Conference call on Feb. 18 at 8:30 AM ET.

data.title: Kenvue Non-GAAP EPS of $0.27 beats by $0.05, revenue of $3.78B beats by $90M
data.description:
• Kenvue press release (KVUE): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.27 beats by $0.05.

data.title: Eminence Capital cuts stake in Advanced Micro Devices, Amazon, adds to Meta, Salesforce in Q4
data.description:
Ricky Sandler's Eminence Capital reduced its position in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Amazon.com (AMZN), and raised its stake in Meta Platforms (META) and Salesforce (CRM) during Q4.

Eminence Capital is a global asset management firm focused on bottom up, fundamental long and short stock picking. The firm has assets under management of ~$6.9B.

The fund cut its holdings in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to ~968K shares from ~1.5M shares, Amazon.com (AMZN) to ~1.48M shares from ~1.55M shares, CF Industries Holdings (CF) to ~1.28M shares from ~1.34M shares, and Dave & Buster's Entertainment (PLAY) to ~2.35M shares from ~2.36M shares.

It trimmed its holdings in Pinterest (PINS) to ~4.5M class A shares from ~6.7M class A shares, Tenet Healthcare (THC) to ~614K shares from ~1.1M shares, MongoDB (MDB) to ~156K class A shares from ~433K class A shares, Zillow (Z) to ~2.1M shares from ~2.5M shares, and CF Industries (CF) to ~1.28M shares from ~1.34M shares.

On the other hand, the New York-based asset manager increased its holdings in Meta Platforms (META) to ~62K class A shares from ~59K class A shares, Salesforce (CRM) to ~1M shares from ~831K shares, Camping World Holdings (CWH) to ~5.78M class A shares from ~3.96M class A shares, and Coupang (CPNG) to ~12.64M class A shares from ~9.34M class A shares.

Additionally, it added to its holdings in DraftKings (DKNG) to 8.4M class A shares from 6.3M class A shares, Peloton Interactive (PTON) to ~18.2M class A shares from ~18.1M class A shares, Sea (SE) to ~2.6M shares from ~1.5M shares, Dynatrace (DT) to ~4.5M shares from ~3.4M shares, GitLab (GTLB) to ~7.7M class A shares from ~7.2M class A shares, Sysco (SYY) to ~897K shares from ~522K shares, and Elevance Health (ELV) to ~487K shares from ~367K shares.

Eminence Capital took a new position in Union Pacific (UNP), having bought ~352K shares during the quarter. It completely exited Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) with the sale of its entire ~4.8M shares, Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) with the sale of its ~1.1M shares, and Seagate Technology (STX) with the sale of ~39K shares, among other trades.

data.title: PSQ Holdings notified by NYSE for non-compliance with continued listing standards
data.description:
• PSQ Holdings (NYSE: PSQH) on Tuesday said that it received a notice from the New York Stock Exchange that it does not meet minimum requirements for total market capitalization, stockholders’ equity, and average closing price for its Class A common stock.
  • The company said the notice does not result in immediate delisting and that it intends to submit a business plan within 45 days to regain compliance with NYSE rules.
  • PSQ Holdings said it can restore compliance with the minimum price rule at any time within a six-month cure period if its stock closes at or above $1.00 on the last trading day of any month and averages at least $1.00 over the prior 30 trading days.
  • The company is considering available options to address the non-compliance.
  • PSQH -0.0% after hours to $0.7754.
  • Source: Press Release
